content: We can frame the LLM self-reflection mechanism as a dynamic, graded vocabulary acquisition process analogous to how high school learners master the concept of 'reflexion' from unfamiliar spelling to internalized reflective thinking practice. Just as language learners gradually refine their understanding of the term 'reflexion' through repeated contextual exposure and active recall, LLMs can refine their self-reflection capabilities by curating memory trajectories that mirror the staged vocabulary learning process.
